steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: ”They are hardly pristine” is an independent clause. The word “though” is a subordinating conjunction, making any clause it introduces dependent on the first clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuating subordinating conjunctions
steps.content:
The keyed punctuation is “pristine, though;”: the comma after “pristine” separates the dependent connector “though”, and the semicolon then breaks before the next clause.

“pristine; though” uses a semicolon before “though”, “pristine, though,” adds an unnecessary comma after “though”, and “pristine, though” omits the semicolon needed before the next clause.
